data.title: UK's Coats Group meets 2025 revenue expectations, expects organic growth in 2026
data.description:
Overview

  • UK apparel components supplier's 2025 revenue met analyst expectations

  • Adjusted operating profit for 2025 missed analyst estimates

  • Company completed acquisition of OrthoLite, enhancing footwear market position

Outlook

  • Coats expects organic growth in 2026 despite market uncertainty

  • OrthoLite to outperform footwear market due to technology penetration

  • Coats anticipates modest organic margin improvement in 2026

Result Drivers

  • PORTFOLIO TRANSITION - Co's exit from non-core Americas Yarns business improved margins by 100bps and enhanced growth potential

  • ORTHOLITE ACQUISITION - Acquisition of OrthoLite accelerated strategy in footwear components, supporting revenue and cash generation

  • RECYCLED THREAD GROWTH - Revenue from 100% recycled threads grew 43% to $554m, reflecting sustainability focus

data.title: ROUNDUP: DHL Group Q4 Results Down, Hikes Dividend, Sees Profit Growth In FY26
data.description:
BONN (dpa-AFX) - German logistics major DHL Group (DPWA.DU), while reporting weak earnings and revenues in its fourth quarter, on Thursday raised dividend, and issued fiscal 2026 outlook, expecting profit growth.

In 2026, the Group expects geopolitical uncertainties to persist.

For fiscal 2026, DHL Group expects an operating profit or EBIT above 6.2 billion euros and free cash flow, excluding M&A, of around 3 billion euros. In fiscal 2025, EBIT was 6.10 billion euros.

The Group expects operating profit over 5.6 billion euros for DHL, and over 0.9 billion euros for Post & Parcel Germany.

Further, the company said its Board of Management and Supervisory Board intend to propose a dividend increase to 1.90 euros per share from last year's 1.85 euros per share, at the upcoming Annual General Meeting.

In the fourth quarter, consolidated net profit dropped 3.4 percent to 1.06 billion euros from 1.10 billion euros. Earnings per share remained flat with last year's 0.93 euro.

EBIT, a key earnings metric, declined 1.3 percent to 1.83 billion euros from 1.85 billion euros.

Revenue for the quarter declined 2.7 percent to 22.09 billion euros from 22.70 billion euros last year.

International revenues were down 4.5 percent to 15.98 billion euros from prior year's 16.72 billion euros.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

data.title: UK's PageGroup's annual pretax profit slumps 67%; flags uncertain outlook
data.description:
British recruiter PageGroup reported a 67% fall in annual pretax profit on Thursday, hurt by prolonged weak hiring in its key European markets, and flagged that the outlook remains uncertain due to an unpredictable economic environment.

Global recruiters are grappling with a hiring slump as weak business confidence and macroeconomic uncertainties have led firms to delay new appointments, and candidates are wary of switching roles amid mounting fears that artificial intelligence could threaten white-collar jobs.

The company, which focuses on hiring white-collar roles, posted an annual pretax of 16.2 million pounds ($21.59 million), compared with 49.1 million pounds a year ago.

($1 = 0.7504 pounds)

data.title: British homebuilder Taylor Wimpey sees lower profit in 2026
data.description:
British homebuilder Taylor Wimpey on Thursday forecast lower profit for 2026, as softer pricing and elevated costs weigh on its business.

Although tax uncertainties eased after the November budget, raising hopes of a recovery in the housing market, homebuilders have remained cautious about 2026 as last year's downturn weighed on year-end order books.

In January, Taylor Wimpey warned that its 2026 profit margin would decline, becoming one of the first major UK homebuilders to flag such a pressure.

The company said it expects 2026 adjusted operating profit to be around 400 million pounds ($533.04 million), compared with 420.6 million pounds reported in the prior year.

Taylor Wimpey expects its performance to be more robust in the second half of the year as it targets UK home completions, excluding joint ventures, of between 10,600 and 11,000 units in 2026, with about 40% of completions in the first half.

($1 = 0.7504 pounds)

data.title: UK's Admiral posts 16% profit jump on customer growth, motor business strength
data.description:
British insurer Admiral Group reported a 16% rise in annual pre-tax profit on Thursday, helped by sustained customer growth and resilient underwriting in its core UK motor insurance business.

The Cardiff-based company posted group pre-tax profit of 957.9 million pounds ($1.28 billion) for the year ended December 31, 2025, compared with 826.5 million pounds a year earlier.

($1 = 0.7502 pounds)

data.title: Bermuda's Lancashire Holdings annual gross premiums rise on strong underwriting results
data.description:
Overview

  • Bermuda insurance provider's gross premiums rose 5.1% to $2.26 bln in 2025

  • Profit after tax for 2025 was $293.4 mln, with ROE of 20.9%

  • Company declared $357 mln total dividends for 2025, including special dividend

Outlook

  • Lancashire expects 2026 to be more competitive but maintains confidence in rate adequacy

  • Company plans to continue investing in Lancashire US and expanding Syndicate 2010 capacity

  • Lancashire's strong capital position and credit rating upgrade support its outlook

Result Drivers

  • UNDERWRITING PROFIT - Strong underwriting results contributed to a $381.1 mln insurance service result with a discounted combined ratio of 83.7%

  • INVESTMENT RETURNS - Total investment return of 7.0% supported overall profitability, contributing $218.0 mln to results

  • CATASTROPHE LOSSES - Despite $277.0 mln in net losses from catastrophe events, including California wildfires, profit after tax was $293.4 mln

data.title: Morgan Stanley does not expect ECB interest rate cuts in 2026
data.description:
Morgan Stanley said on Thursday that it expects the European Central Bank to keep interest rates steady through 2026, citing persistent inflation due to the conflict in the Middle East.

The U.S. bank had previously forecast two rate cuts in June and September this year, but now sees the ECB delivering those reductions in 2027 instead.

data.title: Endeavour Mining's 2025 cash flow rises on high gold prices
data.description:
Overview

  • Gold miner's FY-2025 adjusted EBITDA rose 75%, with adjusted net earnings up 244%

  • Record FY-2025 free cash flow of $1.2 bln, up 269% from FY-2024

  • Company returned $435 mln to shareholders, 93% above minimum commitment

Outlook

  • Endeavour forecasts FY-2026 production of 1,090-1,265 koz at AISC of $1,600-$1,800/oz

  • Company targets minimum $1 bln dividend over 2026-2028, potentially doubling at current gold prices

  • Endeavour aims to discover 12-15 Moz of resources by 2030 at <$40/oz discovery cost

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER GOLD PRICES - Increased realised gold prices significantly boosted margins and cash flows

  • PRODUCTION INCREASE - Full-year commercial production from Sabodala-Massawa BIOX plant and Lafigué mine contributed to higher output

  • EXPLORATION SUCCESS - Increased reserves and resources at Assafou and other sites enhanced growth prospects
